preview_text:
The model favors Portugal at 66%, leaving Uzbekistan with a 9% chance of victory. The sportsbook consensus and Polymarket both shift substantially toward the Portuguese side, pricing Portugal closer to 80%. The underdog faces long odds under all three estimates, but the model assigns it roughly 50% more probability than the market does.

The model's spread suggests a competitive fixture tilted clearly toward Portugal. The draw probability of 25% is material—more than twice the sportsbook's 14%—indicating the model sees meaningful room for a stalemate. The win gap of 41 percentage points between Portugal and Uzbekistan reflects a notable but not overwhelming favorite-underdog split in the model's view.

The model and market diverge meaningfully on this match. The sportsbook consensus and Polymarket are aligned with each other but both assign Portugal roughly 13–14 percentage points more win probability than the model does, while compressing the draw probability. The dashboard does not claim to know which assessment of the match is correct; users may wish to note the discrepancy when deciding how to weight these forecasts.
